discussion_comments.comment_body: This is great! Although, what this does now, is makes it so that if you import and use the `refetchAllQueries` function intentionally, you can't detect errors :(

This block however, is where the window-focus fetching happens. You may have better luck doing something similar there :)

(See below)
I now catch the errors only for the usage of this method instead inside the method, thanks!
Instead of using this new action, just updated the success action below.

The `actionSuccess` handler can look like this: 

```diff
case actionSuccess:
      return {
        ...state,
        status: statusSuccess,
        data: action.data,
        error: null,
        isStale: false,
        isFetching: false,
        canFetchMore: action.canFetchMore,
        updatedAt: Date.now(),
+        failureCount: 0
      }
```
that s is a neat, thanks for reviewing my code :pray:

This test will pass even without this feature implemented:
`retry` is a function, therefore `retry === true` is `false` and `query.state.failureCount <= query.config.retry` is false as well, so query won't be refetched
https://github.com/tannerlinsley/react-query/blob/2b6a711cf38acaa891bf0db3a6d871247abe9b9a/src/queryCache.js#L315-L319

My suggestion is to allow retry once and expect query to fail 2 times
